metadata.author: Moses
metadata.placeOfWriting: On the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho
metadata.subject: Christ Is the Instructor and Leader of the People of God, Able to Prepare Them to Enter the Good Land and Inherit It as Their Possession.

1: These are the words which Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan in the wilderness, in the Arabah, opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Dizahab.
It is eleven days' journey from Horeb by way of Mount Seir to Kadesh-barnea.
And in the fortieth year, in the eleventh month, on the first day of the month, Moses spoke to the children of Israel according to all that Jehovah had commanded him to give to them,
2: Then we turned and set out for the wilderness by way of the Red Sea, as Jehovah had spoken to me; and we went around Mount Seir for many days.
And Jehovah spoke to me, saying,
You have gone around this mountain long enough; turn northward.

3.text: In Deuteronomy, the focus is on Jehovah as the faithful, covenant-keeping God who is training His people to love Him and keep His commandments.
Mount Seir. The command to 'turn northward' signifies a change in direction in the journey of the children of Israel toward the good land.
